mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and housing starts indicate broad-based economic expansion, which lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any rolling 12-month period, or the cross-sectional correlation between INDPRO and HOUST drops below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the federal funds rate and consumer price index trigger a repricing of equity valuations as higher discount rates and inflation uncertainty compress forward earnings multiples, yielding negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression of 20-day returns, or the signal generates positive forward returns in over 55% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth directly expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, generating positive forward returns over a one-week horizon as institutional investors adjust portfolios to reflect stronger macroeconomic momentum.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter change and 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.05 across all available data.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly nonfarm payrolls exceed 150,000 while the unemployment rate simultaneously declines, the labor market demonstrates robust health that supports consumer-driven equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative information coefficient over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not statistically differ from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production coupled with stabilizing core inflation signals improved supply chain efficiency and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates coinciding with an increasing unemployment rate indicate a policy-induced economic slowdown that erodes corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: The interaction effect flips to positive during historical tightening cycles, or the signal fails to predict downward price moves in over 40% of instances.
